meta_title: For Students: debugging with AI to Solve overwhelmed by AI tools (Beginner Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to debugging with AI and overcome overwhelmed by AI tools. A Beginner-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.
h2_structure: The Problem: overwhelmed by AI tools

content.seo_title: Debugging with AI: Master Your First App for Students
content.seo_description: Overwhelmed by AI tools? Learn how to debug code efficiently using Antigravity and Claude Code. A masterclass for students building their first SaaS app.
content.answer_block: Stop fighting the AI noise. Focus on a streamlined workflow: use Antigravity for architectural context and Claude Code for iterative debugging. By isolating bugs into small, logic-based modules, you transform overwhelming errors into manageable tasks. Master this pipeline to ship faster, learn deeper, and build a portfolio that actually works.
content.intro:
You stare at the terminal, a wall of red text mocking your lack of progress. You started with a simple idea, but now you're drowning in a sea of half-baked libraries, hallucinating models, and fragmented documentation. The promise of AI was supposed to make coding easier; instead, it has created a cognitive overload that paralyzes your best intentions.

Take a breath. The gap between your current frustration and a deployed app isn't a lack of intelligence—it's a lack of a systematic debugging framework. By leveraging specialized tools like Antigravity and Claude Code, you can turn your AI assistant from a chaotic suggestion engine into a surgical debugging partner. Let's strip away the noise and get your code running.

steps.title: Isolate the Failure Point
steps.description: Don't ask the AI to fix the whole project. Narrow the scope to the specific function or module throwing the error.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze this file and identify the exact line causing the runtime exception. Explain the logic error in plain English.
steps.claude_code_command: claude read ./src/app.js --focus 'Identify the bug in the authentication middleware'

steps.title: Contextualize the Environment
steps.description: Provide the AI with the necessary context so it doesn't hallucinate non-existent libraries or methods.
steps.antigravity_prompt: I am using Next.js 14 and Supabase. Review this code and suggest a fix that aligns with current best practices for these specific versions.

steps.title: Generate a Unit Test
steps.description: Before fixing, create a test that fails. This proves you understand the bug and prevents regression.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a Jest test case that reproduces this specific bug. Use descriptive assertions.
steps.claude_code_command: claude write 'Create a test file to reproduce the login failure'

steps.title: Apply Surgical Refactoring
steps.description: Use Claude Code to apply the fix directly to your files, maintaining clean code standards.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Propose a refactor for this function that handles null pointers gracefully and adheres to SOLID principles.
steps.claude_code_command: claude apply 'Refactor the handleLogin function to include error checking'

steps.title: Verify and Document
steps.description: Ensure the fix works and update your internal documentation so you don't repeat the mistake.
steps.antigravity_prompt: The fix is working. Add a comment block to this function explaining the edge case we just solved.

pro_tips: Always force the AI to explain its reasoning before it writes code; if it can't explain the 'why', the 'what' is likely a hallucination.
Treat your CLI as your command center; keep Claude Code running in a split-terminal to avoid context switching between browser and IDE.
When stuck, ask the AI to 'act as a senior code reviewer' rather than a coder—this shifts the output toward architectural quality.
Use 'dry-run' flags whenever possible to see the AI's proposed file changes before they overwrite your hard work.
Keep a 'knowledge log' of every bug you solve with AI; reviewing this weekly is the fastest way to become a senior developer.
content.next_steps: Now that you can debug effectively, focus on building a 'Minimum Viable Feature' rather than a full app. Deploy one working component to Vercel today to close the feedback loop.
content.conversion_block: You have the tools, the workflow, and the roadmap. Stop waiting for the 'perfect' time to start building and begin refining your craft through real-world debugging. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.

Before fixing, create a test that fails. This proves you understand the bug and prevents regression.
